entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: no cognition whatsoever without the accompaniment of word

entities.name: pratyaya
entities.cite: There is no cognition in the world that does not follow upon word

entities.name: jñāna
entities.cite: all knowledge appears as if interpenetrated by word

entities.name: anuviddha
entities.cite: anuviddha is the weaver's and jeweller's word, thread through cloth, thread through pearls

entities.name: nirvikalpaka pratyaksa
entities.cite: the Buddhist doctrine that perception proper (nirvikalpaka pratyaksa) is precisely wordless

axioms.name: anugamadapeksam
axioms.cite: no cognition whatsoever without the accompaniment of word

axioms.name: anuviddha
axioms.cite: as if interwoven, shot through, with word

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: Cognition is not first accomplished wordlessly and then labelled; the wording is *in* the seeing, as the thread is in the fabric — remove it and no fabric remains

axioms.name: arthakriyakari
axioms.cite: attend to any actual cognition and the attending is already articulate

denials.name: anugamadapeksam
denials.cite: Against this stands the great counter-tradition — the Buddhist doctrine that perception proper (nirvikalpaka pratyaksa) is precisely wordless

denials.name: utpatti_anantaram_labha
denials.cite: Cognition is not first accomplished wordlessly and then labelled

claims.name: anugamadapeksam
claims.cite: no cognition in the world that does not follow upon word

claims.name: anuviddha
claims.cite: all knowledge appears as if interpenetrated by word

rejected_readings.label: wordless_perception
rejected_readings.rendering: Perception can occur in a wordless state, with conceptualization and verbal articulation added afterwards.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly rejects the Buddhist doctrine of nirvikalpaka pratyaksa (wordless perception), stating that attending to actual cognition reveals it is already articulate. This reading allows a mode of cognition without word dependency.

rejected_readings.label: post_hoc_labeling
rejected_readings.rendering: Knowledge is first independently accomplished, and then subsequently labeled with words.
rejected_readings.why: Commits the dualism the verse and commentary reject: cognition first, word second. The commentary states 'the wording is *in* the seeing, as the thread is in the fabric'; remove the thread and fabric remains is false. Word is constitutive, not post-hoc attribution.

rejected_readings.label: linguistic_description_only
rejected_readings.rendering: Word is merely a tool for describing cognitions that can occur and be known independently of it.
rejected_readings.why: Demotes word to a property (descriptive instrument) rather than recognizing it as constitutive to the structure of all knowing. The sort error (property vs. linguisticItem with constitutive role) treats the relation as contingent rather than necessary.

notes: The verse asserts the intimate co-constitution of all cognition with word. The commentary's metaphor of 'thread in cloth' captures the weaving rather than mere accompaniment; the iva (as if) honors the phenomenological impossibility of introspecting behind articulation, not skepticism about the dependence itself. By 1.124 it becomes constitutive. The denial of nirvikalpaka pratyaksa is direct polemics against Buddhist epistemology.
